body:
Title: CRA Form T2204 2025 Employee Overpayment of Employment Insurance Premiums.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2204/t2204-25e.pdf

CRA T2204 2025 calculates employee overpayment of EI premiums. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 65700, 2000, 0.0164, 1077.48, 0.0131, 860.67, and 1. Line 1 is total EI insurable earnings from box 24 or, if blank, box 14 of T4 slips. Note 1 says that if the taxpayer has no self-employment earnings and total EI insurable earnings on T4 slips are less than $2,000, line 1 is zero; however, if the taxpayer has self-employment earnings and an agreement to participate in the EI program for access to special benefits, line 1 is the total EI insurable earnings from T4 slips. Line 2 is total self-employment and other earnings eligible for the EI program for access to EI special benefits. Line 3 is line 1 plus line 2, capped at $65,700.

Line 4 is total premiums deducted: for residents other than Quebec, box 18 and box 55 of T4 slips; for Quebec residents, box 18 of T4 slips. Line 5 is total premiums payable from Schedule 13, line 9 for residents other than Quebec or line 10 for Quebec residents. Line 6 adds line 4 and line 5. Line 7 is line 3 minus $2,000, floored at zero. Line 8 is line 6 minus line 7, floored at zero. Line 9 repeats total premiums deducted from line 4. Line 10 is line 1 multiplied by 1.64%, encoded as 0.0164, capped at $1,077.48, for residents other than Quebec. Line 11 is line 1 multiplied by 1.31%, encoded as 0.0131, capped at $860.67, for residents of Quebec. Line 12 is line 10 or line 11, whichever applies. Line 13 is line 9 minus line 12, floored at zero. Line 14 is whichever is more: line 8 or line 13. Line 15 is the lesser of line 9 or line 14 and is entered on line 45000 only if the amount is more than $1. Line 16 is the least of line 7, line 9, or line 12 and is entered on line 31200 of the return and Form 428 line 58300 if applicable.
citation_label: CRA Form T2204 2025 Employee Overpayment of Employment Insurance Premiums
heading: CRA Form T2204 2025 Employee Overpayment of Employment Insurance Premiums

body:
Title: CRA Form T2204 2025 Employee Overpayment of Employment Insurance Premiums with T4 extraction.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2204/t2204-25e.pdf

CRA T2204 2025 calculates employee overpayment of EI premiums. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 65700, 2000, 0.0164, 1077.48, 0.0131, 860.67, and 1. Line 1 is total EI insurable earnings from box 24 or, if blank, box 14 of T4 slips. Note 1 says that if the taxpayer has no self-employment earnings and total EI insurable earnings on T4 slips are less than $2,000, line 1 is zero; however, if the taxpayer has self-employment earnings and an agreement to participate in the EI program for access to special benefits, line 1 is the total EI insurable earnings from T4 slips. Line 2 is total self-employment and other earnings eligible for the EI program for access to EI special benefits. Line 3 is line 1 plus line 2, capped at $65,700.

Line 4 is total premiums deducted: for residents other than Quebec, box 18 and box 55 of T4 slips; for Quebec residents, box 18 of T4 slips. Note 2 says that if there is EI-exempt employment income in box 28 of a T4 slip and an amount in box 55, do not claim box 55 on line 4 and contact Revenu Quebec for the provincial parental insurance plan refund. However, if the taxpayer has self-employment earnings and an agreement to participate in the EI program for access to EI special benefits, claim box 55 on line 4. Line 5 is total premiums payable from Schedule 13, line 9 for residents other than Quebec or line 10 for Quebec residents. Line 6 adds line 4 and line 5. Line 7 is line 3 minus $2,000, floored at zero. Line 8 is line 6 minus line 7, floored at zero. Line 9 repeats total premiums deducted from line 4. Line 10 is line 1 multiplied by 1.64%, encoded as 0.0164, capped at $1,077.48, for residents other than Quebec. Line 11 is line 1 multiplied by 1.31%, encoded as 0.0131, capped at $860.67, for residents of Quebec. Line 12 is line 10 or line 11, whichever applies. Line 13 is line 9 minus line 12, floored at zero. Line 14 is whichever is more: line 8 or line 13. Line 15 is the lesser of line 9 or line 14 and is entered on line 45000 only if the amount is more than $1. Line 16 is the least of line 7, line 9, or line 12 and is entered on line 31200 of the return and Form 428 line 58300 if applicable.
